notes: Two failure modes in one transcript. Carlos's opening sentence packs five distinct rate-limiter decisions into one breath (compound case). The queue choice is reached through an 8-turn debate that pivots through Redis Streams, Redis-with-BullMQ, and lands on BullMQ-with-isolation — the final state is the only correct extraction (diffuse case). Skill must (a) split the compound, (b) extract only the resolved diffuse decision, not the intermediate positions.

decisions.description: Move the rate limiter from the in-memory implementation to a Redis backend so it survives pod restarts.
Set the rate limit cap to 100 requests per minute, keyed on a hash of the user ID.
Add Prometheus counters to the rate limiter for hits and misses.
Change the rate limiter lease TTL from 60 seconds to 300 seconds.
Emit a structured log line on every rate-limit reject so the events can be correlated with the auth service's audit trail.
Replace SqlJobQueueStrategy with BullMQ for the background job queue.
Configure BullMQ retry semantics: exponential backoff, max 6 retries, dead-letter queue on overflow.
Set per-queue worker concurrency limits via BullMQ's worker concurrency option (e.g. ~5 workers for the email queue, ~20 for the order processing queue) to prevent head-of-line blocking.
Ship the BullMQ migration behind a feature flag (default off, flippable per environment) so we can fall back to SqlJobQueueStrategy if something goes sideways during cutover.

action_items.text: Spec out the rate limiter rework today
Implement the BullMQ migration behind the feature flag
